prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
The Long-Awaited End
(Sonnet)

At last, the final stroke of midnight chimes,
The long-awaited end to this long race,
A project, once a seedling in my mind,
Now blossomed, bloomed, and left its final trace.

The sleepless nights, the caffeine-fueled fights,
The countless drafts, the endless re-writes,
The self-doubt, the stress, the sleepless nights,
All for this moment, this fleeting height.

The weight of work, at last, has been undone,
The burden lifted, the task complete,
The sense of accomplishment, second none,
A feeling that I can't repeat.

The project's done, the deadline met,
The sigh of relief, a well-earned rest,
The satisfaction, bittersweet,
As I bid farewell to my creation, my quest.

So, here's to the end, the finish line crossed,
A new beginning, a well-deserved loss.
